mundo.titulo: Iran's strike on Israel suggests the regime's sense of resilience is growing
mundo.resumen: Iran's decision to risk jeopardising peace talks may reflect how its leaders view their current position, writes BBC Persian editor Amir Azimi.

mundo.titulo: Starmer tells Apple and Google to ban nude images on children's phones
mundo.resumen: Firms will be expected to activate built-in features to stop children accessing sexually explicit images.

mundo.titulo: Nottingham attacks inquiry revealed miscarriage of justice, victim's mother says
mundo.resumen: The bereaved families of the victims of Valdo Calocane speak at a press conference in London.

mundo.titulo: Rare footage captured of Great White shark in Mediterranean Sea
mundo.resumen: A volunteer diver has described shaking as he filmed his encounter with an endangered Great White shark between Tunisia and Sicily.

mundo.titulo: At least 32 dead after major earthquake strikes southern Philippines
mundo.resumen: The magnitude-7.8 quake triggered small tsunami waves in the Philippines, Indonesia and Japan.

mundo.titulo: Don't try to make James Bond woke, Idris Elba says
mundo.resumen: The British star also said some audiences would not accept a black male actor playing 007.

negocios.titulo: Starmer tells Apple and Google to ban nude images on children's phones
negocios.resumen: Firms will be expected to activate built-in features to stop children accessing sexually explicit images.

negocios.titulo: You may be saving for retirement without realising it. Here's how to check
negocios.resumen: One simple check could ensure you are not missing out on free money which could help in later life.

negocios.titulo: Stock market jitters remain amid tech fears and renewed Middle East attacks
negocios.resumen: Markets in Asia are hit by a tech sell-off, and oil is volatile as Iran and Israel launch attacks on each other.

negocios.titulo: M&S launches new traineeship for 1,000 young people
negocios.resumen: The scheme aims to tackle the "growing challenge" of young people not in employment, education, or training.

negocios.titulo: 'No dead ends': What the Dutch can teach us about tackling youth unemployment
negocios.resumen: The Netherlands has one of the world's lowest rates of 16 to 24-year-olds not in education, employment or training.

negocios.titulo: Spain's visitor numbers hit new highs as tourists avoid Middle East
negocios.resumen: The European country had 9.1 million international visitors in April, the most ever for that month.

tecnologia.titulo: Eventbrite and Vimeo owner Bending Spoons files to go public
tecnologia.resumen: Bending Spoons say its app caters to a user base of over 500 million monthly active users.

tecnologia.titulo: Massachusetts votes to pass new privacy rights bill that bans sale of precise location data
tecnologia.resumen: The bill is expected to blanket ban companies and startups from selling people's precise location data across the state.

tecnologia.titulo: Uber, Wayve and Waymo are headed towards a robotaxi showdown in London
tecnologia.resumen: Uber customers in the UK can now join an interest list to increase their chances of being matched with a Wayve robotaxi.

tecnologia.resumen: We're likely to see more price increases as the big AI companies plan to go public.

tecnologia.resumen: "Chat is dead" — at least, according to a senior OpenAI employee.
